title: Simple assert Helper
instruction: Without a framework, you can write a minimal assert. Define `assert(name, condition)` that logs `"PASS: <name>"` or `"FAIL: <name>"`. Test `add(2, 3) === 5`.

title: Test Multiple Cases
instruction: Good tests cover edge cases. Use your `assert` helper to test `add(0, 0) === 0`, `add(-1, 1) === 0`, and `add(100, 200) === 300`. All should pass.

title: Test Throws
instruction: Test that a function throws by wrapping it in a try-catch. Write `assertThrows(name, fn)` that passes if `fn()` throws. Test `divide(10, 0)` throws.
hint: function assertThrows(name, fn) { try { fn(); console.log("FAIL: " + name); } catch { console.log("PASS: " + name); } }

title: Test Async Functions
instruction: Async functions need an `async` test runner. Write `asyncAssert(name, fn)` that awaits `fn()` and catches errors. Test a function that resolves with 42.
